title: Capture and decode a bus with a logic analyzer
description: Probe the actual wires and decode the protocol before trusting any software-level interpretation of a transaction.
body: Use when a peripheral link misbehaves and the cause is unclear. Do not debug from print statements alone, since they hide wire-level timing and acknowledgement. Actions: attach probes to clock and data lines, trigger on a start or chip-select event, and decode the captured frames against the protocol spec. Validation signals include a decoded trace whose phases match the spec and timing that meets setup and hold requirements. Risk boundary: probing a live high-speed bus can disturb signal integrity, so verify probe loading is acceptable before trusting the capture.
